homepage.heroSubline: We fix cars right the first time, with no hidden costs. Serving Memphis drivers since 2012.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Auto Repair started as a small shop in Midtown Memphis. Our team grew by focusing on clear communication and fair pricing, earning trust across Shelby County.
We approach every repair with diagnostic precision. Before recommending work, we show you the issue and explain options. No upsells, just what your vehicle actually needs.
Memphis roads and weather take a toll on cars. We're committed to keeping local vehicles safe, whether it's a Beale Street cruiser or a family SUV in Germantown.

valueProps.title: No Guesswork
valueProps.description: We diagnose issues with modern scanners before making recommendations, so you pay only for necessary repairs.

valueProps.title: Upfront Pricing
valueProps.description: Get a detailed estimate before we turn a wrench. No surprises when you pick up your vehicle.

valueProps.title: Local Parts Network
valueProps.description: Our relationships with Memphis-area suppliers mean quality parts without long waits or inflated costs.

valueProps.title: Loaner Cars
valueProps.description: Need wheels while we work? Ask about our free loaner vehicles for qualifying repairs.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-AUT-R92 for same-day diagnostics. Open Monday-Saturday.

services.name: Brake Repair
services.shortDescription: Reliable brake inspections and repairs for Memphis stop-and-go traffic.
services.description:
Memphis drivers know the wear of constant braking on Poplar Avenue or I-240. We test brake pads, rotors, calipers and fluid with road conditions in mind.

Our process starts with measuring pad thickness and checking for uneven wear. Warped rotors common in Tennessee's humid climate get resurfaced or replaced. We bleed systems to remove moisture that corrodes components.

Expect a written report with photos showing your brake condition. We only recommend replacements when safety is compromised, never just for upsells. All work includes a 12-month warranty.
process: Step 1: Road test to assess pedal feel and noises
Step 2: Measure pad thickness and rotor wear
Step 3: Inspect calipers and brake lines for leaks
Step 4: Perform repairs or recommend replacements
benefits: Quieter stops with properly bedded pads
Even braking pressure across all wheels
Reduced risk of fluid boiling in summer heat
Longer component life with proper servicing

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers replace brake pads?
faq.answer: Every 30,000-50,000 miles depending on your commute. Downtown stop-and-go traffic wears pads faster than highway driving.

faq.question: Can you resurface rotors instead of replacing them?
faq.answer: Yes, if they meet minimum thickness specs. We measure precisely before recommending either option.

services.name: AC Repair
services.shortDescription: Cooling system repairs for Memphis' humid summers.
services.description:
When your AC blows warm air on a 95-degree Memphis afternoon, we find the cause fast. Our technicians check refrigerant levels, compressors, condensers and electrical components.

Tennessee humidity accelerates corrosion in AC systems. We pressure-test for leaks and inspect condenser fins for damage from road debris. Recharges include UV dye to pinpoint future leaks.

For older vehicles, we often recommend upgrading to R134a refrigerant if still using R12. This improves cooling efficiency and avoids obsolete refrigerant costs. All repairs come with a summer-ready guarantee through September.
process: Step 1: Performance test with manifold gauges
Step 2: Inspect compressor clutch and electrical connections
Step 3: Check condenser and evaporator for leaks
Step 4: Recharge system with precise refrigerant amounts

faq.question: Why does my AC work sometimes but not others?
faq.answer: This often indicates a failing compressor clutch or electrical issue. We test these components under load to diagnose intermittent problems.

faq.question: How long does an AC recharge last?
faq.answer: Properly sealed systems should last 2-3 years. If it leaks out sooner, we'll find the source under warranty.

services.name: Transmission Service
services.shortDescription: Fluid changes and repairs to keep your transmission shifting smoothly.
services.description:
Memphis' mix of highway driving and city traffic demands reliable transmissions. We perform fluid exchanges using manufacturer-approved fluids and filters.

For problematic transmissions, our diagnostic scanners read manufacturer-specific trouble codes that generic readers miss. We check solenoid operation, fluid condition, and torque converter function before recommending rebuilds.

Severe service intervals apply for most Memphis drivers due to heat and traffic. We recommend fluid changes every 30,000 miles for automatic transmissions, sooner if you tow or haul heavy loads.
process: Step 1: Scan for diagnostic trouble codes
Step 2: Check fluid level and condition
Step 3: Test shift solenoids and pressure
Step 4: Perform flush or recommend repairs

faq.question: Should I flush or drain my transmission fluid?
faq.answer: Flushes clean the entire system but aren't suitable for neglected transmissions. We assess contamination levels first.

faq.question: What causes hard shifting in Memphis heat?
faq.answer: Old fluid loses viscosity when hot. We test for this and check for sticking valves in the valve body.

services.name: Suspension Repair
services.shortDescription: Shock, strut, and alignment services for Memphis roads.
services.description:
Potholes on Summer Avenue and uneven bridge transitions wear out suspension components faster. We inspect shocks, struts, control arms and bushings for Memphis-specific wear patterns.

Our alignment machine accounts for Tennessee's crowned roads and railroad crossings. We adjust to manufacturer specs unless you prefer slight modifications for better handling.

Common repairs include ball joints damaged by potholes and struts leaking after years of thermal cycling. We use corrosion-resistant hardware to combat road salt from occasional winter storms.
process: Step 1: Road test to identify noises or handling issues
Step 2: Lift inspection of all suspension components
Step 3: Measure wheel alignment angles
Step 4: Replace worn parts and verify repair effectiveness

faq.question: How do I know if my shocks need replacing?
faq.answer: Look for excessive bouncing after bumps or fluid leaks. Memphis potholes often accelerate wear.

faq.question: Why does my car pull after hitting a pothole?
faq.Answer: This likely indicates bent suspension components or misalignment. We check for both.

services.name: Battery Service
services.shortDescription: Battery testing and replacements for Tennessee's temperature swings.
services.description:
Memphis' hot summers and occasional freezing winters strain car batteries. We load-test batteries to measure real capacity, not just voltage. This catches weak batteries before they strand you.

For replacements, we install batteries with higher cold-cranking amps (CCA) than minimum requirements. This provides extra margin for winter starts. All installations include terminal cleaning and corrosion treatment.

Electrical system testing comes standard. We check alternator output and parasitic draws that drain batteries prematurely. Many late-model vehicles need computer resets after battery changes - we handle these protocols properly.
process: Step 1: Load test battery under simulated conditions
Step 2: Check charging system voltage and ripple
Step 3: Test for parasitic draws when vehicle is off
Step 4: Install new battery with proper registration if needed
benefits: Properly sized battery for your vehicle

faq.question: How long do car batteries last in Memphis?
faq.answer: Typically 3-4 years due to heat accelerating internal corrosion. We see more failures in July and January.

faq.question: Can a bad alternator kill a new battery?
faq.answer: Yes. That's why we always test the charging system during battery service.

reviews.name: James W.
reviews.text: The team at Apex fixed my squealing brakes on a 2017 F-150. They showed me the worn pads and explained why the rotors needed resurfacing. Final price matched the estimate exactly. My truck stops smoothly now, even pulling a boat out of Shelby Forest.

reviews.name: Latasha R.
reviews.text: After my AC quit on I-40 near the stadium exit, Apex diagnosed a failed compressor clutch same-day. They had the part by noon next day and didn't push unnecessary services. Cool air works better than when I bought my Civic used.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: My Silverado started slipping gears between Germantown and downtown. Apex found the transmission fluid was burned from towing in summer heat. Fluid change and cooler line flush fixed it for half what the dealer wanted.

reviews.name: Diane K.
reviews.text: When my Subaru wouldn't start after that cold snap in January, Apex tested the battery properly instead of just replacing it. Turned out to be a bad starter connection they fixed for minimal cost. Honest work.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: They aligned my Challenger after I hit a pothole on Winchester Rd. Not only fixed the pull but explained how Memphis roads affect camber settings. Threw in a free tire rotation too.

reviews.name: Elena S.
reviews.text: The guys at Apex recharged my minibus' AC before our family road trip. They found a slow leak at the condenser and fixed it properly instead of just topping off. Kids stayed cool all the way to Nashville.

Whether you're driving through downtown or commuting to work, our expert technicians are here to keep your vehicle running smoothly. We specialize in everything from routine maintenance to complex repairs, ensuring your car stays reliable on the road.

Memphis is known for its rich musical heritage and bustling streets, which means your vehicle needs to be in top condition. From brake repairs to engine diagnostics, our team uses the latest tools and technology to provide top-notch service. Trust Apex Auto Repair to get you back on the road quickly and safely.
